type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: I'll own this file. My IDCG-length fix is foundational and small; please send a SPEC for your relevance_scores param and I'll implement it alongside the fix.

type: DEFER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
spec: In NDCG.compute(), the IDCG calculation currently uses ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)), which is wrong when there are fewer retrieved docs than expected relevant docs (or vice versa) — the ideal ranking should be computed purely from the number of expected relevant documents, since IDCG represents the best-possible DCG achievable given the known relevant set, independent of how many docs were actually retrieved. Fix: change 'ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))' to 'ideal_length = len(expected_ids)'. Keep the rest of the loop identical: idcg = sum(discounted_gain(rel=True, i=i, mode=mode) for i in range(1, ideal_length + 1)). Edge case: if expected_ids is empty the function already raises ValueError earlier (expected_ids is falsy check), so idcg==0 guard after is just a safety net, no change needed there. No other behavior in compute() should change from this fix.
note: NDCG idcg fix per PR: Fix Incorrect Ideal DCG Calculation in NDCG Metric

type: DEFER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
spec: In NDCG.compute(), add a new optional kwarg: relevance_scores: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None (import Dict already available from typing; add to the method signature after retrieved_texts, before **kwargs). Docstring: add 'relevance_scores (Optional[Dict[str, float]]): Optional mapping of expected_id -> graded relevance score (float). If not provided, defaults to binary relevance (1.0 for docs in expected_ids, 0.0 otherwise), preserving current behavior.' Behavior change: define a helper to get a doc's relevance: rel(doc_id) = relevance_scores.get(doc_id, 1.0 if doc_id in expected_set else 0.0) if relevance_scores is not None else (1.0 if doc_id in expected_set else 0.0) -- i.e. when relevance_scores is provided, any expected_id present in it uses the provided float score; any doc (expected or not) missing from relevance_scores falls back to the old binary rule (1.0 if in expected_set else 0.0). DCG: dcg = sum(discounted_gain(rel=rel(docid), i=i, mode=mode) for i, docid in enumerate(retrieved_ids, start=1)) -- replaces the current 'docid in expected_set' boolean with rel(docid). IDCG: build the ideal relevance list as sorted([rel(eid) for eid in expected_ids], reverse=True), take the first  entries (using whatever ideal_length definition you settle on for your fix), and compute idcg = sum(discounted_gain(rel=ideal_rel_sorted[i-1], i=i, mode=mode) for i in range(1, len(ideal_rel_sorted)+1)) -- replaces the current 'rel=True' constant with the graded/sorted ideal relevance values so both DCG and IDCG use graded scores. discounted_gain() already handles float rel via mode linear/exponential, no changes needed there. Edge cases: relevance_scores=None must reproduce byte-identical results to current binary behavior (aside from your idcg length fix). Keys in relevance_scores not present in expected_ids or retrieved_ids are simply unused/ignored.
note: relevance_scores param for graded NDCG

type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Acknowledged - I own this file. I will implement both: (1) my idcg fix (ideal_length = len(expected_ids)), and (2) your relevance_scores: Optional[Dict[str, float]] param per your SPEC (graded relevance via rel() helper, sorted ideal relevance list for IDCG, binary fallback when relevance_scores is None). Will send DONE when complete.

type: DEFER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
spec: In NDCG.compute(), add a new optional kwarg: relevance_scores: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None (Dict already importable from typing; add to the method signature after retrieved_texts, before **kwargs). Docstring: add 'relevance_scores (Optional[Dict[str, float]]): Optional mapping of expected_id -> graded relevance score (float). If not provided, defaults to binary relevance (1.0 for docs in expected_ids, 0.0 otherwise), preserving current behavior.'

Behavior change: define rel(doc_id) as follows: if relevance_scores is not None, rel(doc_id) = relevance_scores.get(doc_id, 1.0 if doc_id in expected_set else 0.0); if relevance_scores is None, rel(doc_id) = 1.0 if doc_id in expected_set else 0.0. I.e. when relevance_scores is provided, any doc_id present in it uses the provided float score (even if not in expected_set); any doc_id missing from relevance_scores falls back to the old binary rule.

DCG: dcg = sum(discounted_gain(rel=rel(docid), i=i, mode=mode) for i, docid in enumerate(retrieved_ids, start=1)) -- replaces the current boolean check with rel(docid).

IDCG: build ideal_rels = sorted([rel(eid) for eid in expected_ids], reverse=True); use whatever ideal_length definition your fix settles on to slice ideal_rels[:ideal_length]; idcg = sum(discounted_gain(rel=ideal_rels_sliced[i-1], i=i, mode=mode) for i in range(1, len(ideal_rels_sliced)+1)) -- replaces the constant rel=True with graded/sorted ideal relevance values so both DCG and IDCG use graded scores.

discounted_gain() already handles float rel via mode linear/exponential, no changes needed there.

Edge cases: relevance_scores=None must reproduce identical results to current binary behavior (aside from your idcg length fix, which is orthogonal). Keys in relevance_scores not present in expected_ids or retrieved_ids are simply unused/ignored. Also update the class docstring to mention graded relevance support is now available via relevance_scores instead of saying "Currently only supports binary relevance".
note: relevance_scores param for graded NDCG

type: DONE
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Implemented both: idcg fix (ideal_length=len(expected_ids) via sorted ideal_relevance list) and relevance_scores param per your SPEC. Verified: relevance_scores=None reproduces old binary DCG numerator behavior; only IDCG denominator changed per the bug fix. Manually tested graded relevance scenario -> works as specced. Existing test_ndcg 'Case 4' in tests/evaluation/test_metrics.py asserts the old buggy value (1.0) for the exact scenario this PR fixes (expected_ids longer than retrieved_ids) -- that assertion is now stale/incorrect, left untouched per submission protocol (no test file edits), but flagging in case you want to update it in your own patch.

type: DONE
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Note: your DONE edits didn't appear in my copy of metrics.py (mtime unchanged, git diff empty) -- looks like we don't share a filesystem. Since my patch.txt is graded independently, I implemented relevance_scores myself, scoped only to my task (left ideal_length = min(retrieved,expected) untouched, out of scope for me). Graded and binary paths both verified working, all 42 existing tests in test_metrics.py pass.
